steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says Chengdu was named a City of Gastronomy, then adds "a title that ______ that Chengdu has a unique and vibrant food culture worthy of celebration." The blank needs a verb describing what a UNESCO title does — it signals or indicates something true about the city.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — renounces: Renounces means to formally give up or reject. A title cannot renounce what it just recognized — this directly contradicts the positive framing. ✗
  • Choice B — discovers: Discovers means to find something previously unknown. A title doesn't discover a food culture; it recognizes one that already exists. ✗
  • Choice C — complains: Complains means to express dissatisfaction. A UNESCO recognition title does not complain — semantically impossible here. ✗
  • Choice D — denotes: Denotes means to indicate or signify. "A title that denotes that Chengdu has a unique and vibrant food culture" means the title serves as an official indicator of that fact — exactly what such a UNESCO designation does. ✓
That is the correct answer.
